text: The laparoscopic inspection reveals significant sigmoid diverticulitis with pericolonic inflammatory adhesions, a small interloop abscess, and frank intraabdominal purulent fluid. The surgeon drains the purulent fluid and irrigates the abdomen with an antibiotic solution, noting a microperforation of the sigmoid diverticulitis without gross fecal contamination.

The surgeon performs a primary repair of the perforated diverticulitis using intracorporeal suturing with multiple intracranial sutures and a two-hole etabond technique, aiming to eliminate the need for a temporary colostomy.
